questions.correctValue: A chunk of text — such as a word, part of a word, or punctuation mark — that the model processes as a single unit
questions.explanation: Tokens are the atomic units LLMs use to process text. They are neither single characters nor full sentences — they are variable-length text chunks determined during tokenization. Understanding tokens helps students grasp context window limits and why models sometimes struggle with unusual words.

questions.topic: Why role context improves LLM responses
questions.correctValue: It helps the model select an appropriate vocabulary, depth, and tone for the intended audience
questions.explanation: Role context provides the model with a frame of reference for calibrating vocabulary, tone, and depth. It does not change the model's knowledge base, extend context limits, or prevent hallucination — it only shapes how information is communicated.

questions.topic: What a hallucination is and why it happens
questions.explanation: Hallucination is not intentional deception. It is a byproduct of the next-token prediction mechanism — the model generates plausible-sounding text without a built-in fact-checking process. The model has no awareness that it has produced incorrect information.

questions.correctValue: Prompt B, because it specifies the audience, length, topic focus, and format, reducing ambiguity
questions.explanation: Prompt B is superior because it constrains the output along four dimensions: audience, length, subject focus, and writing style. Specificity reduces the model's degrees of freedom and guides it toward a response that is actually useful. Prompt A's brevity is a weakness, not a feature.

questions.correctValue: Option B, because he is using AI as a thinking and planning tool while doing the writing and verification work himself
questions.explanation: Option B demonstrates appropriate AI use: the student employs AI as a scaffolding tool, verifies the output for accuracy, and does the intellectual work of writing independently. Option A constitutes academic dishonesty because it substitutes AI output for the student's own learning and effort.
